A construction supplier provides less material than paid for using deceptive measures.

title: Counterfeiting Government Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for forging or counterfeiting government-issued stamps.
section_text: This section deals with counterfeiting government stamps. It penalizes individuals who forge or counterfeit stamps issued by the government for public or official use.
example_scenarios: A person produces fake revenue stamps to sell illegally.
An individual creates counterfeit court fee stamps for document forgery.

title: Possessing Counterfeiting Instruments for Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for possessing instruments to counterfeit government stamps.
section_text: This section penalizes individuals who possess instruments or materials used for counterfeiting government stamps.
example_scenarios: Police find stamp printing plates and special inks in a suspect's possession.
An individual is caught with equipment used to create fake government stamps.

title: Making or Possessing Counterfeit Government Stamp

chapter.chapter_name: Offences relating to Documents and to Property Marks

description: Deals with the act of making or possessing a counterfeit government stamp.
section_text: Section 257 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the act of making or possessing a counterfeit government stamp.
legal_elements: The instrument is for the purpose of making a counterfeit stamp
The stamp is a government stamp
example_scenarios: A person is found with a printing press specifically designed to create fake revenue stamps for legal documents.
An individual is caught manufacturing fake postal stamps in their home to sell on the black market.

title: Possessing Counterfeit Government Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for possessing counterfeit stamps with fraudulent intent.
section_text: This section criminalizes possessing counterfeit government stamps with the intent to use them for fraudulent purposes.
example_scenarios: A person is found with a large quantity of fake revenue stamps.
An individual possesses counterfeit court fee stamps intended for illegal use.

title: Using Counterfeit Government Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for knowingly using counterfeit stamps in transactions.
section_text: This section deals with knowingly using counterfeit government stamps in legal or financial transactions.
example_scenarios: A person uses fake revenue stamps on property documents.
An individual presents documents with counterfeit stamps to a government office.

title: Tampering with Government Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for erasing or altering genuine stamps for reuse.
section_text: This section punishes the act of erasing or altering a genuine government stamp with intent to reuse it fraudulently.
example_scenarios: A person removes cancellation marks from used stamps to reuse them.
An individual chemically alters a used stamp to make it appear new.

title: Removing Government Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for unauthorized removal of stamps from documents for reuse.
section_text: This section penalizes the unauthorized removal of a government stamp from any document to be used again.
example_scenarios: A person carefully removes a revenue stamp from a document to reuse it.
An individual detaches court fee stamps from old documents for fraudulent use.

title: Possessing Fraudulently Removed Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for possessing stamps fraudulently removed from documents.
section_text: This section penalizes the act of knowingly possessing a government stamp that has been fraudulently removed.
example_scenarios: A person is found with stamps that were removed from official documents.
An individual possesses revenue stamps known to be fraudulently detached.

title: Using Fraudulently Removed Stamps

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Documents and Property Marks

description: Punishment for using stamps fraudulently removed in transactions.
section_text: This section punishes the use of a fraudulently removed government stamp in transactions.
example_scenarios: A person uses a stamp removed from another document on a new agreement.
An individual presents documents with fraudulently removed stamps to authorities.

title: Fraudulent Use of False Weighing Instruments

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Weights and Measures

description: Punishment for fraudulent use of false instruments for weighing goods.
section_text: This section penalizes fraudulent use of a false instrument for weighing goods.
example_scenarios: A shopkeeper uses a manipulated weighing scale to cheat customers.
A trader uses false weights while selling commodities.

title: Possessing False Weights with Fraudulent Intent

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Weights and Measures

description: Punishment for possessing false weights or measures with intent to defraud.
section_text: This section penalizes the fraudulent possession of a false weight or measure with the intent to use it for deception.
example_scenarios: A merchant keeps underweight weights to use during sales.
A business owner stores altered measuring instruments.

title: Using False Weights or Measures

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Weights and Measures

description: Punishment for knowingly using false weights or measures for fraud.
section_text: This section punishes individuals who knowingly use false weights or measures for fraudulent purposes.
example_scenarios: A fuel station owner uses tampered pumps to dispense less fuel.
A grain merchant uses false weights while selling to customers.

title: Making or Selling False Weights

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Weights and Measures

description: Punishment for making, selling, or disposing of false weights or measures.
section_text: This section penalizes individuals who make, sell, or dispose of false weights or measures with the intent to defraud.
example_scenarios: A factory produces underweight weights for sale to shopkeepers.
A business sells altered measuring tapes to traders.

title: Public Nuisance

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Definition of public nuisance causing injury, danger or annoyance to public.
section_text: This section defines 'Public Nuisance' as an act or illegal omission that causes injury, danger, or annoyance to the public.
example_scenarios: A factory releases toxic fumes affecting the entire neighborhood.
A person blocks a public road causing traffic congestion.

title: Negligent Spread of Infectious Disease

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for negligent acts likely to spread infectious diseases.
section_text: This section penalizes negligent acts that are likely to spread infectious diseases dangerous to life.
example_scenarios: A person with contagious illness knowingly goes to crowded public places.
A hospital negligently disposes of medical waste spreading infection.

title: Malignant Spread of Infectious Disease

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for malignant spread of infectious disease endangering public health.
section_text: This section penalizes the act of malignantly spreading an infectious disease dangerous to life.
example_scenarios: A person knowingly infected with a serious disease deliberately coughs on others.
A bioterrorist releases infectious agents in a public space.

title: Negligent Spread of Infectious Disease

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for knowingly or negligently spreading infectious diseases.
section_text: This section penalizes the act of knowingly or negligently spreading or attempting to spread an infectious disease, such as a contagious virus, in a reckless manner.
example_scenarios: A person with contagious illness knowingly attends a crowded event without precautions.
A hospital negligently fails to isolate patients with infectious diseases.

title: Adulterated Food or Drink

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for manufacturing, selling, or distributing adulterated food or drink.
section_text: This section penalizes individuals who manufacture, sell, or distribute adulterated food or drink meant for human consumption.
example_scenarios: A milk producer adds water and chemicals to increase volume.
A restaurant uses spoiled ingredients to prepare meals for customers.

title: Selling Unsafe Food or Drink

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for selling or possessing unsafe food or drink that may harm health.
section_text: This section addresses the offense of selling, distributing, or possessing unsafe food or drink that may pose a danger to human health.
example_scenarios: A shopkeeper sells expired canned goods knowing they may be harmful.
A distributor supplies contaminated bottled water to retailers.

title: Adulterated Drugs or Medicines

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for manufacturing or selling adulterated drugs or medicines.
section_text: This section penalizes individuals who manufacture or sell adulterated drugs or medicines that may be harmful to consumers.
example_scenarios: A pharmaceutical company produces medicines with incorrect ingredients.
A pharmacist sells counterfeit drugs that contain harmful substances.

title: Selling Adulterated Drugs

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for selling adulterated drugs or medicines that are harmful.
section_text: This section deals with the selling of adulterated drugs or medicines that have been tampered with or are harmful to human health.
example_scenarios: A medical store sells fake antibiotics that contain no active ingredients.
A dealer distributes adulterated painkillers with dangerous additives.

title: Manufacturing Adulterated Drugs or Food

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for manufacturing or selling adulterated drugs or food with intent to harm.
section_text: This section penalizes the act of manufacturing or selling adulterated drugs or food with the intent to cause harm to consumers.
example_scenarios: A factory produces baby food with harmful contaminants intentionally.
A company manufactures drugs with known dangerous side effects.

title: Water Pollution

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for corrupting or fouling water sources for public use.
section_text: This section penalizes individuals or entities that pollute or corrupt water sources intended for public use.
example_scenarios: A factory discharges untreated chemical waste into a river used for drinking water.
A person dumps sewage into a public water reservoir.

title: Atmospheric Pollution

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for making atmosphere noxious to health.
section_text: This section penalizes acts that make the atmosphere noxious to health.
example_scenarios: A factory releases toxic fumes affecting the entire neighborhood.
Burning hazardous waste creates dangerous air pollution in a residential area.

title: Rash Driving

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for reckless or negligent driving endangering human life.
section_text: This section punishes reckless or negligent driving that endangers human life.
example_scenarios: A driver speeds through a crowded market area ignoring traffic signals.
A person drives under the influence of alcohol in a residential neighborhood.

title: Rash Navigation of Vessel

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for rash navigation of vessel endangering lives.
section_text: This section penalizes rash navigation of a vessel, endangering lives.
example_scenarios: A ship captain navigates through a storm without proper safety measures.
A boat operator speeds through a crowded waterway ignoring safety protocols.

title: False Vehicle Information

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for providing false information about vehicle to public officer.
section_text: Section 281 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the act of providing a false information about a vehicle's identity, ownership, or driver's license to a public officer.
example_scenarios: A person provides fake registration documents during a traffic stop.
An individual gives false information about vehicle ownership to police.

title: Sale of Adulterated Food or Drink

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for selling or distributing adulterated food or drink.
section_text: Section 282 of the Indian Penal Code penalizes the sale or distribution of food or drink that is adulterated or unsafe for human consumption.
example_scenarios: A restaurant serves food prepared with contaminated ingredients.
A beverage company distributes drinks with harmful additives.

title: Obstructing Public Way

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for endangering safety by obstructing public roads or spaces.
section_text: Section 283 of the Indian Penal Code criminalizes the act of endangering the safety of others by obstructing public roads, rivers, or other public spaces.
example_scenarios: A shopkeeper extends their shop display onto the sidewalk blocking pedestrian access.
Construction materials are left on a public road causing traffic obstruction.

title: Negligent Handling of Dangerous Substances

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for negligent acts endangering life through hazardous substances.
section_text: Section 284 of the Indian Penal Code pertains to negligent acts likely to endanger human life or the safety of others, particularly through the handling or use of hazardous substances.
example_scenarios: A worker carelessly stores flammable chemicals in a crowded area.
A laboratory technician mishandles toxic substances without proper safety measures.

title: Negligent Conduct with Dangerous Substances

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for negligent conduct endangering life, health, or property.
section_text: Section 285 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the act of negligent conduct involving dangerous substances or materials that could endanger human life, health, or property.
example_scenarios: A person stores explosives in a residential building without safety precautions.
A factory worker negligently handles chemicals causing a hazardous spill.

title: Negligent Handling of Explosives

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for negligent handling of explosive substances.
section_text: Section 286 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the act of negligently handling explosive substances or materials likely to cause harm to life, property, or the public.
example_scenarios: A construction worker carelessly stores dynamite near residential areas.
A pyrotechnics operator mishandles fireworks during a public event.

title: Negligent Handling of Machinery

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for negligent handling of dangerous machinery.
section_text: Section 287 of the Indian Penal Code concerns the negligent act of handling machinery or other mechanical objects that are dangerous or likely to cause harm to others.
example_scenarios: A factory operator runs heavy machinery without safety guards.
A construction worker operates dangerous equipment without proper training.

title: Negligent Maintenance of Infrastructure

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for negligent maintenance endangering public safety.
section_text: Section 288 of the Indian Penal Code pertains to negligent acts that endanger public safety by not ensuring the proper maintenance of public infrastructure or buildings.
example_scenarios: A building owner ignores structural cracks that endanger occupants.
A municipality fails to maintain a public bridge showing signs of collapse.

title: Negligent Keeping of Dangerous Animal

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for negligent keeping of dangerous animal endangering safety.
section_text: Section 289 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the negligent act of keeping a dangerous animal that endangers the safety of others or public property.
example_scenarios: A person keeps a venomous snake in an unsecured enclosure in a residential area.
A zoo fails to properly secure dangerous animals putting visitors at risk.

title: Public Nuisance

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for committing public nuisance disturbing peace or safety.
section_text: Section 290 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the act of committing a public nuisance, such as engaging in conduct that harms or disturbs public peace or safety.
example_scenarios: A person creates excessive noise late at night disturbing the neighborhood.
Someone blocks a public pathway with personal belongings causing inconvenience.

title: Continuance of Public Nuisance

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for continuing public nuisance after warning.
section_text: Section 291 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the continuance of a public nuisance after a warning or order to cease such activities.
example_scenarios: A person continues loud construction work after police warning to stop.
Someone persists in blocking a public road despite official orders to clear it.

title: Sale of Obscene Material

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for selling, distributing, or circulating obscene material.
section_text: Section 292 of the Indian Penal Code criminalizes the sale, distribution, and circulation of obscene material, including books, films, or other visual representations.
example_scenarios: A shopkeeper sells pornographic magazines to the public.
An individual distributes obscene films through digital platforms.

title: Sale of Obscene Material to Minors

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for selling or distributing obscene material to minors.
section_text: Section 293 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the sale and distribution of obscene material to minors.
example_scenarios: A bookstore owner sells adult magazines to underage customers.
A person shares explicit content with minors through social media.

title: Obscene Acts in Public

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Affecting the Public Health, Safety, Convenience, Decency and Morals

description: Punishment for committing obscene acts or singing obscene songs in public.
section_text: Section 294 of the Indian Penal Code criminalizes the act of committing obscene acts or singing obscene songs in public places.
example_scenarios: A person performs lewd acts on a public street.
An individual sings sexually explicit songs in a crowded park.

title: Defilement of Place of Worship
description: Punishment for destroying or defiling place of worship to hurt religious feelings.
section_text: Section 295 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the deliberate destruction or defilement of a place of worship with the intent to hurt religious feelings.
example_scenarios: A person vandalizes a temple with offensive graffiti.
Someone deliberately damages religious artifacts in a church.

title: Disturbing Religious Assembly
description: Punishment for disturbing religious assemblies or meetings.
section_text: Section 296 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the act of disturbing religious assemblies or meetings.
example_scenarios: A person creates loud noise during a church service to disrupt prayers.
Someone interrupts a mosque congregation with offensive remarks.

title: Trespassing in Burial or Religious Place
description: Punishment for trespassing into burial place or religious site with intent to desecrate.
section_text: Section 297 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the act of trespassing into a burial place or a place of religious worship with the intent to insult the dead or desecrate the place.
example_scenarios: A person enters a cemetery at night and damages graves.
Someone trespasses into a temple with intent to steal religious artifacts.

title: Insulting Religious Feelings
description: Punishment for uttering words or gestures to insult religious feelings.
section_text: Section 298 of the Indian Penal Code deals with uttering words or making gestures with the intent to insult the religious feelings of any person.
example_scenarios: A person makes derogatory comments about another's religion in public.
Someone uses offensive hand gestures during a religious procession.

title: Culpable Homicide
description: Definition of culpable homicide causing death with intent to harm.
section_text: Section 299 of the Indian Penal Code defines the offense of culpable homicide, which is the causing of death with the intention of causing harm.
example_scenarios: A person hits another during a fight, causing fatal injury without intent to kill.
A driver causes death by reckless driving but without murderous intent.

title: Murder
description: Definition of murder as unlawful killing with intent to cause death.
section_text: Section 300 of the Indian Penal Code defines murder, the most severe form of homicide, where the death of a person is caused with the intent to kill.
example_scenarios: A person plans and executes a killing for financial gain.
Someone intentionally shoots another person with the clear intent to cause death.

title: Murder During Unlawful Act
description: Murder committed with intention of causing death while committing unlawful act.
section_text: Section 301 of the Indian Penal Code defines the offense of murder committed with the intention of causing death, as well as when the killing occurs while committing an unlawful act.
legal_elements: direct unlawful act leading to death
example_scenarios: A person commits robbery and intentionally kills a security guard during the crime.
During a kidnapping, the abductor kills the victim to avoid being caught.

title: Murder
description: Punishment for intentionally causing death of another person.
section_text: Section 302 of the Indian Penal Code defines the offense of murder and prescribes the punishment for it.
example_scenarios: A person plans and executes a killing for revenge.
Someone intentionally poisons another person with the clear intent to cause death.

title: Murder by Life Convict
description: Punishment for murder committed by person serving life sentence.
section_text: Section 303 of the Indian Penal Code addresses murder committed by life convicts. This section applies when a person serving a life sentence commits murder.
example_scenarios: A prisoner serving life sentence kills a fellow inmate.
A life convict murders a prison guard during an escape attempt.

title: Culpable Homicide Not Amounting to Murder
description: Punishment for causing death without murder intent but with knowledge of potential death.
section_text: Section 304 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offense of culpable homicide not amounting to murder.
example_scenarios: A person gets into a fight and hits another, causing fatal injury without intent to kill.
A driver causes death by reckless driving but without murderous intent.

title: Abetment of Suicide by Child or Insane Person
description: Punishment for abetting suicide of minor or insane person.
section_text: Section 305 of the Indian Penal Code defines the crime of abetment of a child or an insane person to commit suicide.
example_scenarios: An adult encourages a depressed teenager to commit suicide.
A caregiver convinces a mentally ill person to end their life.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Abetment of Suicide by Vulnerable Person

title: Abetment of Suicide
description: Punishment for abetting or inciting another person to commit suicide.
section_text: Section 306 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the offense of abetment of suicide. It applies when someone encourages or incites another person to take their own life.
example_scenarios: A person constantly bullies another until they commit suicide.
Someone provides means and encouragement for a depressed person to end their life.

title: Attempted Murder
description: Punishment for attempting to kill another person with intent.
section_text: Section 307 of the Indian Penal Code deals with attempted murder, where the accused tries to kill another person but fails in the attempt.
example_scenarios: A person shoots at someone but misses, with clear intent to kill.
Someone tries to poison another but the victim survives after medical treatment.

title: Attempt to Commit Culpable Homicide
description: Punishment for attempting culpable homicide without intent to kill.
section_text: Section 308 of the Indian Penal Code pertains to the offense of attempting to commit culpable homicide, where the intention is not to kill but to cause harm that could potentially lead to death.
example_scenarios: A person attempts to seriously injure another knowing it could cause death.
Someone tries to push another from height without intent to kill but with knowledge of risk.

title: Attempt to Commit Suicide
description: Punishment for attempting to commit suicide.
section_text: Section 309 of the Indian Penal Code defines the offense of attempting suicide and prescribes the punishment for such an act.
example_scenarios: A person tries to overdose on medication but is saved by medical intervention.
Someone attempts to jump from a building but is prevented by rescuers.

title: Abetment of Suicide by Vulnerable Person
description: Punishment for abetting suicide of minor, insane person, or person under duress.
section_text: Section 310 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offense of abetment to commit suicide by a minor, insane person, or a person under duress or distress.
example_scenarios: An adult convinces a mentally ill family member to commit suicide.
Someone pressures a minor under their care to end their life.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Abetment of Suicide by Vulnerable Person

title: Causing Death by Negligence
description: Punishment for causing death through negligence or recklessness.
section_text: Section 311 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offense of causing death by means of negligence or recklessness, which could lead to harm or even death, without intent to kill.
example_scenarios: A doctor's gross negligence during surgery causes patient death.
A construction worker's careless handling of equipment leads to fatal accident.

title: Causing Miscarriage Without Consent
description: Punishment for causing miscarriage or abortion without woman's consent.
section_text: Section 312 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offense of causing miscarriage or abortion without the woman's consent.
example_scenarios: A person administers abortion drugs to a woman without her knowledge.
A doctor performs abortion on a patient who did not consent to the procedure.

title: Causing Miscarriage Without Consent
description: Punishment for causing miscarriage without woman's consent.
section_text: This section punishes the act of causing a miscarriage without the woman's consent, making it a serious offense.
example_scenarios: A husband forces his wife to undergo abortion against her will.
A medical practitioner performs abortion on a patient without obtaining proper consent.

title: Death During Illegal Abortion
description: Punishment for causing woman's death during illegal abortion.
section_text: This section penalizes individuals who cause the death of a woman while performing an illegal abortion.

punishment.imprisonment: up to 10 years or life imprisonment

example_scenarios: An unqualified person performs abortion leading to patient's death.
A back-alley abortion clinic causes fatal complications due to unsafe practices.

title: Preventing Child Birth or Causing Death After Birth
description: Punishment for preventing child birth or causing death after birth.
section_text: This section punishes acts done with the intent to prevent a child from being born alive or to cause it to die after birth, unless done in good faith to save the mother's life.
example_scenarios: A person prevents a child from being born alive during delivery.
Someone causes death of a newborn immediately after birth to hide the birth.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Preventing Birth or Causing Neonatal Death

title: Causing Death of Child in Womb
description: Punishment for causing death of child in womb by deliberate act.
section_text: This section punishes the act of causing the death of a child in the womb by a deliberate act, making it a serious offense.
example_scenarios: A person intentionally injures a pregnant woman causing fetal death.
Someone administers harmful substances to cause miscarriage of a viable fetus.

title: Abandoning Child Under 12
description: Punishment for abandoning child under age of 12.
section_text: This section criminalizes the act of abandoning a child under the age of 12, especially if done by the child's parents or guardians.
example_scenarios: Parents leave their 8-year-old child alone on the streets.
Guardians abandon a 10-year-old child at a railway station.

title: Causing Death of Newborn and Concealing Birth
description: Punishment for causing death of newborn and concealing birth.
section_text: This section deals with the act of causing the death of a child born alive, but the child is subsequently disposed of to hide the fact of its birth.
example_scenarios: A mother causes death of her newborn and hides the body.
Someone kills a baby immediately after birth and conceals the birth from authorities.

title: Causing Hurt Resulting in Permanent Disability
description: Punishment for intentionally causing injury resulting in permanent disability.
section_text: This section addresses the act of intentionally causing harm to a person's body to inflict injury or harm that may result in permanent disability.
example_scenarios: A person intentionally breaks someone's spine causing permanent paralysis.
Someone causes eye injury resulting in permanent blindness during an assault.

title: Definition of Grievous Hurt
description: Defines grievous hurt including permanent disfigurement or loss of function.
section_text: This section defines grievous hurt, including permanent disfigurement, loss of sight, or any serious injury that affects the body's function or structure.

title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt not amounting to grievous hurt.
section_text: This section pertains to the offense of voluntarily causing hurt to another person, which is less severe than grievous hurt.
example_scenarios: A person slaps another during an argument causing minor injury.
Someone punches another resulting in bruises but no permanent damage.

title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt.
section_text: This section punishes the act of voluntarily causing grievous hurt to another person.
example_scenarios: A person intentionally breaks someone's arm during a fight.
Someone causes permanent eye damage by throwing a sharp object.

title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Hurt
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt without grievous injury.
section_text: This section addresses the punishment for voluntarily causing hurt. It is a lesser offense compared to grievous hurt, but still a serious violation of personal safety.
example_scenarios: A person pushes another causing minor injuries in a dispute.
Someone hits another with a light object causing bruises.

title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt by Dangerous Weapons
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt using dangerous weapons.
section_text: This section pertains to the punishment for voluntarily causing hurt by dangerous weapons or means, such as knives, stones, or other dangerous instruments.
example_scenarios: A person attacks another with a knife causing injuries.
Someone throws a heavy object at another person causing harm.

title: Punishment for Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt.
section_text: This section addresses the punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt, which causes harm to a person that results in severe injuries or permanent damage.
example_scenarios: A person intentionally causes severe head injury during an attack.
Someone deliberately breaks multiple bones causing permanent disability.

title: Voluntarily Causing Grievous Hurt by Dangerous Weapons
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt using dangerous weapons.
section_text: This section deals with voluntarily causing grievous hurt by dangerous weapons or means, including severe injury inflicted with firearms, knives, or any other dangerous items.
example_scenarios: A person shoots another causing life-threatening injuries.
Someone attacks with a machete causing severe permanent damage.

title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt for Extortion
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt to extort money or compel action.
section_text: This section punishes voluntarily causing hurt to extort money or to compel a person to do something.
example_scenarios: A person beats someone to force them to pay protection money.
Someone injures another to compel them to sign a property document.

title: Causing Hurt by Administering Substances
description: Punishment for causing hurt by administering harmful substances.
section_text: This section punishes the act of causing hurt by administering substances with the intent to cause injury or harm to the victim.
example_scenarios: A person secretly adds harmful substance to someone's drink.
Someone administers drugs to another person to cause injury.

title: Voluntarily Causing Hurt for Extortion
description: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt with extortion intent.
section_text: This section penalizes the act of voluntarily causing hurt with the intention to extort money or force the victim to act in a particular way.
example_scenarios: A person injures someone to force them to hand over money.
Someone causes harm to compel another to perform an illegal act.

title: Causing Hurt to Extract Confession
description: Punishment for causing hurt to extract confession or information.
section_text: This section applies to the act of causing hurt to extract confessions or information, often in police custody or under duress.
example_scenarios: Police officers beat a suspect to extract confession.
Someone tortures another to obtain secret information.

title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confession or information

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt with the intent of obtaining a confession or information.
section_text: This section deals with the punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt to extort confessions or information.
legal_elements: Intent to extort a confession or information
example_scenarios: A police officer severely beats a suspect in custody to force a confession about a crime.
A private security guard tortures an employee to extract information about stolen company property.

title: Causing hurt to public servant on duty

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for causing hurt to a public servant while they are performing their official duties.
section_text: This section covers causing hurt to a public servant while performing their duties, which includes any form of violence or injury inflicted upon officials.
legal_elements: While such public servant is discharging their duty
example_scenarios: A person punches a traffic police officer who is issuing a challan.
During a protest, a demonstrator throws a stone that injures a municipal worker cleaning the area.

title: Causing grievous hurt to public servant on duty

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for causing grievous hurt to a public servant while they are performing their official duties.
section_text: This section applies to causing grievous hurt to a public servant while they perform their duties, with greater penalties for harm inflicted on officials.
legal_elements: While such public servant is discharging their duty
example_scenarios: A person attacks a revenue officer with a blunt object during a property survey, causing a fracture.
A mob leader orders an assault on a forest officer, resulting in permanent eye damage to the officer.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Grievous hurt to deter public servant

title: Voluntarily causing hurt on provocation

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for voluntarily causing hurt with the intention to insult or provoke a person.
section_text: This section involves punishment for voluntarily causing hurt on a person with the intention of insulting or provoking them.
legal_elements: With the intention to insult or provoke
example_scenarios: A person slaps another after being insulted about their family in a public argument.
During a heated cricket match, a player throws a ball intentionally to hit an opponent who taunted him.

title: Voluntarily causing grievous hurt on provocation

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for voluntarily causing grievous hurt with the intention to insult or provoke a person.
section_text: This section deals with the voluntary causing of grievous hurt with the intention to insult or provoke a person, leading to a more serious consequence than a simple hurt.
legal_elements: With the intention to insult or provoke
example_scenarios: In a fit of rage after a severe insult, a person hits another with an iron rod, causing a broken arm.
A driver, provoked by road rage, rams his vehicle into another car, causing serious injuries to the other driver.

title: Act endangering life or personal safety of others

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for acts that endanger the life or personal safety of others through reckless or negligent behavior.
section_text: This section applies to acts that endanger life or personal safety of others through reckless or negligent behavior.
legal_elements: As to endanger human life or personal safety of others
example_scenarios: A person fires a gun in a crowded market for fun, risking lives.
A construction supervisor ignores safety protocols, allowing workers to operate without helmets near heavy machinery.

title: Causing hurt by act endangering life or personal safety

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for causing hurt to another person through negligent or reckless conduct that endangers life.
section_text: This section punishes acts that cause hurt to another person due to negligent or reckless conduct that endangers life.
legal_elements: Doing any act rashly or negligently
As to endanger human life or personal safety
example_scenarios: A drunk driver crashes into a cyclist, causing minor injuries.
A factory owner fails to maintain machinery, and a worker's hand gets caught, causing lacerations.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Hurt by rash or negligent act

title: Causing grievous hurt by act endangering life or personal safety

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for causing grievous hurt to another person through reckless or negligent behavior.
section_text: This section applies to acts that cause grievous hurt to others through negligent or reckless behavior.
legal_elements: Doing any act rashly or negligently
As to endanger human life or personal safety
Causing grievous hurt to any person
example_scenarios: A speeding truck driver loses control and hits a pedestrian, causing multiple fractures.
A chemist negligently stores explosive chemicals which ignite, causing severe burns to a customer.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Grievous hurt by rash or negligent act

title: Wrongful restraint

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Definition and punishment for wrongful restraint, where a person wrongfully restricts the liberty of another.
section_text: This section deals with wrongful confinement, where a person wrongfully restricts the liberty of another person.
legal_elements: In which that person has a right to proceed
example_scenarios: A shopkeeper blocks the exit of a customer, accusing them of shoplifting without evidence.
During a dispute, a person stands in a doorway to prevent another from leaving a room.

title: Wrongful confinement

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Definition and punishment for wrongful confinement.
section_text: This section applies when someone is confined by a person acting with lawful authority or under any legal right, but it is done without just cause.
legal_elements: In such a manner as to prevent that person from proceeding beyond certain circumscribing limits
example_scenarios: A landlord locks a tenant inside a room over a rent dispute.
A security guard detains a visitor in a store's back office without proper cause or authority.

title: Punishment for wrongful restraint

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful restraint.
section_text: This section deals with wrongful restraint, where a person wrongfully prevents someone from proceeding in any direction they have a right to go.
example_scenarios: A person is physically blocked from entering their own property by a neighbor.
Protesters chain themselves to a gate, preventing officials from entering a government building.

title: Punishment for wrongful confinement

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement.
section_text: This section applies to the act of wrongful confinement, which is more serious than wrongful restraint, where a person is confined within any limit against their will.
example_scenarios: A person is locked in a basement by kidnappers for a short period.
An employer locks an employee in the office overnight as punishment for a mistake.

title: Wrongful confinement for three or more days

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement for a period of three days or more.
section_text: This section applies when a person is wrongfully confined for a period of 3 days or more.
example_scenarios: A person is held captive in a remote farmhouse for four days over a financial dispute.
A family member locks up a mentally ill relative in a room for a week without seeking medical help.

title: Wrongful confinement for ten or more days

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement for a period exceeding ten days.
section_text: This section punishes wrongful confinement for a period exceeding 10 days, treating it as a more severe offense.
example_scenarios: A person is kidnapped and held in a secluded location for two weeks for ransom.
A cult leader confines followers in a compound for several weeks, isolating them from the outside world.

title: Wrongful confinement in a house or place

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement in a house or a place.
section_text: This section applies to wrongful confinement in a house or a place where a person is confined and is deprived of their freedom to move.
example_scenarios: A husband locks his wife inside their apartment during a domestic dispute.
A shop owner detains a suspected shoplifter in a storage room.

title: Wrongful confinement with intent to cause harm or inconvenience

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement with an intent to cause harm or inconvenience to the victim.
section_text: This section pertains to wrongful confinement of a person for a certain period, leading to more serious legal consequences.
legal_elements: With intent to cause harm or inconvenience
example_scenarios: A person locks a rival businessman in an office to prevent them from attending an important meeting.
A landlord confines a tenant in a room to coerce them into signing an unfair lease agreement.

title: Wrongful confinement with intent to cause harm

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement with intent to cause harm to the individual.
section_text: This section punishes the wrongful confinement of a person by causing harm or threatening to do so. The confinement is done with a malicious intent to harm the individual.
example_scenarios: A person kidnaps and confines a rival, threatening physical violence if they do not comply with demands.
A gang member confines an informant in a warehouse with the intent to torture them for information.

title: Wrongful confinement affecting physical or mental well-being

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement that negatively impacts a person’s physical and mental health.
section_text: This section applies to cases of wrongful confinement where a person is confined in a manner that directly affects their physical and mental well-being.
legal_elements: In a manner likely to cause harm to their physical or mental health
example_scenarios: A person is confined in a dark, damp cellar without food or water, causing severe anxiety and physical weakness.
An employer locks an employee in a small, unventilated room for hours, leading to a panic attack.

title: Wrongful confinement to a particular area

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement to a particular area or space without consent.
section_text: This section deals with the act of wrongfully confining a person to a particular area or space without their consent or knowledge.
legal_elements: To a particular area or space
example_scenarios: A farmer confines a trespasser to a shed on his property instead of calling the police.
A teacher locks a misbehaving student in a classroom after school hours.

title: Wrongful confinement to coerce an act

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for wrongful confinement with the intent to coerce the victim into doing something.
section_text: This section imposes penalties on individuals who wrongfully confine others with intent to force them to perform a certain act or action.
legal_elements: With intent to compel that person to do something against their will
example_scenarios: A loan shark confines a debtor until they agree to sign over property as collateral.
A person locks a family member in a room to force them to withdraw money from the bank.

title: Assault

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Definition and punishment for assault, which is an act intended to cause fear or injury.
section_text: Section 351 of the Indian Penal Code defines the offence of assault, which is an act intended to cause fear or injury to another person.
legal_elements: Intending or knowing it to be likely that such gesture or preparation will cause any person present to apprehend that the person making it is about to use criminal force
example_scenarios: A person raises a fist and threatens to hit another during an argument, causing fear.
A driver gets out of his car and advances aggressively towards another driver after a minor accident.

title: Punishment for assault or criminal force otherwise than on grave provocation

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for using force against someone without their consent, but without intent to cause grievous harm.
section_text: Section 352 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the offence of using force against someone without their consent, but without the intent to cause serious injury.
legal_elements: Assaults or uses criminal force to any person
Otherwise than on grave and sudden provocation
example_scenarios: A person pushes another during a minor altercation in a queue.
A shopkeeper shoves a customer out of his shop after a disagreement.

title: Assault or criminal force to deter public servant from duty

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for assaulting or using threats to prevent a public servant from performing their duty.
section_text: Section 353 of the Indian Penal Code deals with using force or intimidation to deter a public servant from performing their duty.
legal_elements: To any person being a public servant
In the execution of his duty as such public servant, or with intent to prevent or deter him from discharging his duty
example_scenarios: A person threatens a tax inspector with a stick to stop them from surveying a property.
A crowd pelts stones at police officers trying to disperse an unlawful assembly.

title: Assault or criminal force to woman with intent to outrage her modesty

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for assaulting or using criminal force with the intent to outrage the modesty of a woman.
section_text: Section 354 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the offence of assault or the use of criminal force with the intent to outrage the modesty of a woman.
legal_elements: Assaults or uses criminal force to any woman
example_scenarios: A man touches a woman inappropriately on a crowded bus.
A person makes obscene gestures and tries to grab a woman walking on the street.

title: Assault or criminal force with intent to dishonour person

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for using assault or force to dishonor or humiliate someone.
section_text: Section 355 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offence of assault or criminal force used with the intent to dishonor or humiliate someone.
legal_elements: Assaults or uses criminal force to any person
Intending thereby to dishonour that person
example_scenarios: A person slaps another in public to insult and lower their social standing.
A group forcibly applies paint on someone's face and parades them to humiliate them.

title: Assault or criminal force in attempt to commit theft of property carried by a person

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for using force to rob or commit theft against another person.
section_text: Section 356 of the Indian Penal Code criminalizes the act of assaulting or using criminal force on someone with the intent to rob or commit theft.
legal_elements: Assaults or uses criminal force to any person
In attempting to commit theft of any property which that person is then wearing or carrying
example_scenarios: A thief pushes a pedestrian to the ground and snatches their mobile phone.
A person uses force to grab a gold chain from someone's neck.

title: Assault or criminal force in attempt wrongfully to confine a person

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for assaulting or using force to extort money or property from a person.
section_text: Section 357 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offence of assault or the use of force to extort money or property from a person.
legal_elements: Assaults or uses criminal force to any person
In attempting wrongfully to confine that person
example_scenarios: A kidnapper uses force to try and drag a victim into a vehicle.
A person assaults a neighbor while trying to lock them in a room over a dispute.

title: Assault or criminal force on grave provocation

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Punishment for assault or using criminal force with the intent to outrage or disturb public tranquility.
section_text: Section 358 of the Indian Penal Code criminalizes the act of assault or using criminal force with the intent to outrage or disturb the public tranquility.
legal_elements: Assaults or uses criminal force to any person
example_scenarios: A person, upon discovering their spouse in an act of adultery, assaults the other person involved.
A father assaults a man who was caught molesting his child.

title: Kidnapping

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Definition of kidnapping.
section_text: Section 359 of the Indian Penal Code criminalizes the act of kidnapping and abduction of a person.
legal_elements: Out of the keeping of the lawful guardian of such person
Without the consent of such guardian, if the person is under sixteen years of age if male, or under eighteen years of age if female

title: Kidnapping from India

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting the Human Body

description: Definition of kidnapping from India.
section_text: Section 360 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offence of kidnapping a person from India, or from a lawful guardian or friend.
legal_elements: Conveys any person beyond the limits of India
Without the consent of that person, or of some person legally authorized to consent on behalf of that person

title: Kidnapping from Lawful Guardianship

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Deals with the offence of kidnapping a minor or a person under lawful guardianship.
section_text: Section 361 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offence of kidnapping from lawful guardianship.
legal_elements: Taking or enticing a minor (under 16 for males, under 18 for females) or a person of unsound mind
From the lawful guardianship of another
Without the consent of the guardian
example_scenarios: A man lures a 14-year-old boy away from his parents' home with promises of video games, intending to keep him away.
A woman takes her neighbor's 6-year-old daughter from a playground without informing the parents.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Kidnapping to Compel Marriage

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Punishes kidnapping with the intent of compelling the victim to marry against their will.
section_text: Section 362 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the act of kidnapping with the intent of compelling the victim to marry against their will.
legal_elements: With the intention of compelling that person to marry against their will
example_scenarios: A man kidnaps a woman from her workplace to force her into marriage to settle a family dispute.
A group abducts a young man to compel him to marry a particular woman against his wishes.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Kidnapping to Take Out of India

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Addresses kidnapping with the intent to take the person outside India.
section_text: Section 363 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the crime of kidnapping a person with the intent to cause them to be taken outside of India.
legal_elements: With the intent to take that person outside the limits of India
example_scenarios: A trafficking ring kidnaps a person with the plan to transport them to a neighboring country for forced labor.
A person kidnaps their own child during a custody dispute with the intention of fleeing the country.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Kidnapping or Abducting to Murder

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Deals with kidnapping or abducting a person with the intent to murder or cause grievous harm.
section_text: Section 364 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the offence of kidnapping or abducting a person with the intent to kill or harm them.
legal_elements: With the intent to murder that person or cause them grievous hurt
example_scenarios: A person is abducted for ransom, and the kidnappers intend to kill the victim if the ransom is not paid.
A rival gang member is kidnapped with the explicit plan to cause them serious, life-threatening injury.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Kidnapping to Secretly Confine

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Deals with kidnapping a person with the intent to secretly confine them.
section_text: Section 365 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the crime of kidnapping a person with the intent to secretly confine them.
legal_elements: With the intent to secretly and wrongfully confine that person
example_scenarios: A person is kidnapped and locked in a remote basement with no one else knowing their location.
An individual is abducted and held in a secluded farmhouse to extort money from their family.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Kidnapping Woman to Compel Marriage or Illicit Intercourse

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Punishes kidnapping or abducting a woman to force her into marriage or illicit intercourse.
section_text: Section 366 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the act of kidnapping or abducting a woman with the intention of forcing her to marry or to illicitly engage in sexual activity.
legal_elements: With the intent to compel her to marry against her will
OR with the intent to force or seduce her to illicit intercourse
example_scenarios: A woman is abducted from a bus stop with the intention of forcing her into a marriage with her abductor.
A gang kidnaps a woman with the plan to sell her into prostitution.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Kidnapping of Woman for Sexual Exploitation

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Kidnapping to Subject to Grievous Hurt, Slavery, etc.

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Punishes kidnapping or abducting to subject a person to grievous hurt, slavery, or exploitation.
section_text: Section 367 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the act of kidnapping or abducting a person with the intent to subject them to grievous harm, slavery, or any form of illegal exploitation.
legal_elements: With the intent to subject that person to grievous hurt
OR to the unnatural lust of any person
OR to any other illegal and immoral purpose
example_scenarios: A person is kidnapped with the intent to force them into bonded labor at a brick kiln.
An individual is abducted to be subjected to torture or severe physical punishment.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Inducing to Commit Kidnapping for Confinement or Sexual Exploitation

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Deals with inducing a person to perform acts punishable under Section 365 or 366.
section_text: Section 368 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the punishment for inducing a person to perform an act that is punishable under Section 365 or Section 366, with the intent of secret confinement or forced sexual activity.
legal_elements: To do any act which constitutes an offence under Section 365 or Section 366
example_scenarios: A person hires or convinces someone else to kidnap a victim for the purpose of secret confinement.
An individual provides a drug to a woman, intending that another person will kidnap and sexually assault her while she is incapacitated.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Kidnapping Child Under Ten to Sell

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Punishes kidnapping or abducting a child under ten with the intent to sell them.
section_text: Section 369 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the act of kidnapping or abducting a child under the age of ten with the intention of selling them.
legal_elements: Child is under the age of ten years
With the intent to sell or dispose of the child
example_scenarios: A newborn is stolen from a hospital with the intention of selling the baby to a childless couple.
A young child is lured from a park with candy, with the kidnapper planning to sell the child to a begging ring.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Trafficking of Person

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Addresses the crime of trafficking a person for exploitation.
section_text: Section 370 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the crime of trafficking a person, especially for exploitation, including forced labor or prostitution.
legal_elements: For purposes of exploitation (e.g., forced labor, sexual exploitation, slavery)
example_scenarios: A person is recruited from a village with false promises of a job in the city, but is then forced into prostitution.
A network transports individuals across state borders to work in inhuman conditions in a factory without pay.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Habitual Dealing in Slaves

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Deals with the crime of habitually buying, disposing of, or dealing in slaves.
section_text: Section 371 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the crime of buying or disposing of a person as a slave, and the punishment associated with this offense.
legal_elements: Includes buying, selling, disposing of, or trafficking in persons as slaves
example_scenarios: A person runs a business that regularly procures and sells individuals as domestic slaves to wealthy clients.
An individual is part of a network that transports and trades people for bonded labor in agricultural fields.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Selling Minor for Prostitution

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Punishes selling a minor for the purpose of prostitution or exploitation.
section_text: Section 372 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the act of selling a minor for the purpose of exploitation, including for prostitution or forced labor.
legal_elements: Selling or letting for hire a minor
With the intent that such minor shall be employed or used for prostitution
OR for illicit intercourse with any person
OR for any unlawful and immoral purpose
example_scenarios: A poverty-stricken parent is paid to hand over their teenage daughter to a brothel owner.
A person sells a young boy to a pedophile ring.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Selling of Minor for Sexual Exploitation

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Buying Minor for Prostitution

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Punishes purchasing or hiring a minor for sexual exploitation or forced labor.
section_text: Section 373 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the act of purchasing or hiring a person under the age of eighteen for sexual exploitation or forced labor.
legal_elements: Buying, hiring, or otherwise obtaining possession of a minor
With the intent that such minor shall be employed or used for prostitution
OR for illicit intercourse with any person
OR for any unlawful and immoral purpose
example_scenarios: A brothel owner purchases a 16-year-old girl from a trafficker to work in his establishment.
A man hires a young boy from an intermediary with the intent of sexually abusing him.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Buying of Minor for Sexual Exploitation

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Unlawful Compulsory Labor

chapter.chapter_name: Offences Relating to Kidnapping, Abduction, Slavery and Forced Labour

description: Deals with the crime of unlawful compulsory labor (forced labor).
section_text: Section 374 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the crime of unlawful compulsory labor, also known as forced labor, and punishes anyone who forces an individual into such labor.
legal_elements: Unlawfully compelling any person to labor against their will
example_scenarios: A landlord forces a debtor to work on his farm without wages to repay a loan.
A factory owner locks workers inside and forces them to work overtime without pay or the freedom to leave.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Definition of Rape

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting The Human Body

description: Defines the crime of rape and lays down the punishment for sexual assault without consent.
section_text: Section 375 of the Indian Penal Code defines the crime of rape and lays down the punishment for the act of sexual assault without consent, or when the consent is obtained under force, fraud, or threat.
legal_elements: Or with consent obtained by putting her or any person in fear of death or hurt
Or with consent given under a misconception of fact
Or with consent given by a woman of unsound mind or intoxicated
Or with a woman under 18 years of age (with or without consent)

punishment.imprisonment: not less than 7 years, may extend to life

example_scenarios: A man has sexual intercourse with a woman after threatening to harm her child.
A doctor has sexual intercourse with a patient by falsely claiming it is part of a medical treatment.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Punishment for Rape

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting The Human Body

description: Defines and punishes the crime of rape, including aggravated forms.
section_text: Section 376 of the Indian Penal Code defines and punishes the crime of rape, including the aggravated form of rape, and specifically deals with the punishment for different categories of offenders.
legal_elements: Commission of rape as defined under Section 375
Aggravating circumstances may include: rape by police officer, public servant, armed forces, on pregnant woman, etc.

punishment.imprisonment: not less than 7 years, may extend to life

example_scenarios: A police officer rapes a woman who has come to the police station to file a complaint.
A group of men collectively rape a woman.
A man rapes his 12-year-old daughter.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Unnatural Offences

chapter.chapter_name: Of Offences Affecting The Human Body

description: Deals with unnatural offenses or sexual acts against the order of nature.
section_text: Section 377 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the crime of unnatural offenses or sexual acts against the order of nature, particularly non-consensual acts between persons of the same sex or involving animals.
legal_elements: Carnal intercourse against the order of nature
With any man, woman, or animal
example_scenarios: A man forces another man to engage in non-consensual anal sex.
A person has sexual intercourse with an animal.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Definition of Theft
description: Defines the crime of theft.
section_text: Section 378 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the crime of theft, specifically the unlawful taking of another person's property with the intent to permanently deprive the owner of it.
legal_elements: Property taken out of the possession of another person
With the intent to permanently deprive the owner of it
example_scenarios: A person takes a bicycle parked outside a shop without the owner's permission, intending to keep it.
A shoplifter conceals a book in their bag and walks out of a bookstore without paying.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Punishment for Theft
description: Punishes the crime of theft.
section_text: Section 379 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the crime of theft specifically in cases of personal property and items stolen from individuals or businesses.
legal_elements: Commission of theft as defined in Section 378
example_scenarios: A person is caught and convicted for stealing a wallet from a pedestrian's pocket.
A thief is sentenced for breaking into an unlocked car and stealing the stereo.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Theft in Dwelling House, etc.
description: Punishes theft committed in a dwelling house, shop, or building.
section_text: Section 380 of the Indian Penal Code punishes theft in a dwelling house, shop, or building, emphasizing theft committed in places where people reside or work.
legal_elements: Committed in a building, tent, or vessel used as a human dwelling, or for the custody of property
example_scenarios: A thief enters a house through an open window at night and steals jewelry.
A person sneaks into a closed shop and steals cash from the register.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Theft by Clerk or Servant
description: Deals with theft committed by a servant or employee in the course of employment.
section_text: Section 381 of the Indian Penal Code deals with theft committed by a servant or clerk or employee of a person, corporation, or government body, in the course of their employment.
legal_elements: Committed by a clerk or servant
Property stolen is in the possession of the master or employer
example_scenarios: A cashier at a store steals money from the cash drawer.
A domestic servant steals jewelry from the employer's bedroom.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Theft After Preparation for Hurt, Restraint or Fear
description: Punishes theft committed with an attempt to cause death or grievous harm.
section_text: Section 382 of the Indian Penal Code punishes the act of theft with an attempt to cause death or grievous harm to the victim.
legal_elements: The offender, at the time of committing theft, has made preparation for causing death, hurt, or restraint, or fear of death/hurt/restraint to any person
example_scenarios: A thief carries a knife while stealing a purse, prepared to use it if the victim resists.
A person commits theft after tying up the watchman of a property.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Definition of Extortion
description: Defines the crime of extortion.
section_text: Section 383 of the Indian Penal Code penalizes the crime of extortion, where the offender compels a person to give up property, money, or benefits through the use of threats.
legal_elements: Intentionally putting a person in fear of injury to that person or another
Thereby dishonestly inducing that person to deliver any property, valuable security, or anything signed/sealed which may be converted into a valuable security
example_scenarios: A person threatens to publish embarrassing photos unless they are paid money.
A local goon threatens a shopkeeper with vandalism unless a monthly 'protection' fee is paid.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Punishment for Extortion
description: Addresses the crime of extortion by putting a person in fear of injury.
section_text: Section 384 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the crime of extortion committed by putting a person in fear of injury in order to obtain property or money.
legal_elements: Commission of extortion as defined in Section 383
example_scenarios: A person is convicted and sentenced for threatening a businessman to pay money or face physical harm.
A convict receives a prison term for blackmailing a colleague over stolen office supplies.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Putting Person in Fear of Injury to Commit Extortion
description: Deals with putting a person in fear of injury to commit extortion.
section_text: Section 385 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the crime of putting a person in fear of injury to commit extortion.
legal_elements: Putting or attempting to put any person in fear of injury
With the intent to commit extortion
OR to aid in the committing of extortion
example_scenarios: A person sends a threatening letter demanding money, but is apprehended before any payment is made.
Someone confronts a victim and threatens them with harm unless they agree to pay, but the victim immediately reports it to the police.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Extortion by Putting in Fear of Death or Grievous Hurt
description: Deals with extortion by putting a person in fear of death or grievous harm.
section_text: Section 386 of the Indian Penal Code deals with the act of extortion by putting a person in fear of death or grievous harm with the intention of extorting money or property.
legal_elements: By putting the victim in fear of death or of grievous hurt to that victim or another
example_scenarios: A kidnapper demands ransom from a family, threatening to kill the kidnapped victim if not paid.
A person extorts money by threatening to cause serious, permanent injury to the victim's child.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Extortion by Threat of Injury to Person or Property
description: Penalizes extortion involving threatening a person with harm.
section_text: Section 387 of the Indian Penal Code penalizes extortion that involves threatening a person with harm in order to compel them to deliver property or money, using threats of violence.
legal_elements: Putting or attempting to put any person in fear of injury
example_scenarios: A person threatens to beat up a shop owner unless they pay a weekly sum.
An individual sends a message threatening to damage a car unless a payment is made.

court_judgment_links.summary: Placeholder for a relevant Supreme Court judgment.

title: Extortion by Threat of Accusation of Offence
description: Deals with extortion by threatening to accuse someone of an offense.
section_text: Section 388 of the Indian Penal Code specifically deals with extortion where the extortionist threatens to accuse someone of an offense to cause damage to their reputation, in order to extract money or property.
legal_elements: Threatening to accuse a person of an offence
With intent to cause harm to that person's reputation
And thereby extorting property or valuable security
example_scenarios: A person threatens to falsely accuse a businessman of fraud unless a large sum of money is paid.
An individual threatens to publicly accuse someone of adultery unless they hand over a property deed.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Extortion by Threat of False Accusation

title: Putting Person in Fear of Accusation of Offence to Commit Extortion
description: Addresses putting someone in fear of harm to extort money or goods.
section_text: Section 389 of the Indian Penal Code addresses the crime of putting someone in fear of harm to their person or property and thereby extorting money or goods.
legal_elements: Putting or attempting to put a person in fear of an accusation of an offence
With the intent to commit extortion
example_scenarios: A person sends a draft letter falsely accusing a victim of a crime, demanding money not to send it.
Someone meets a victim and outlines a false accusation they will make unless paid, but the victim refuses and reports it.

crime_type_mapping.crime_name: Attempted Extortion by Threat of Accusation
